core.label: C Fundamentals
core.description: Compilation, basic syntax, data types, and program structure.

topics.label: Compilation & Toolchain
topics.description: Compile C programs with gcc/clang, understand preprocessing, compiling, assembling, and linking stages.

topics.label: Hello World & Program Structure
topics.description: Understand main(), #include, return values, and basic program layout. Every C program starts here.

topics.label: Data Types & Variables
topics.description: int, char, float, double, and their sizes. Understanding data types is critical for memory-aware programming.

topics.label: Operators & Expressions
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison, logical, bitwise, and assignment operators. These form all computation in C.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch, for, while, do-while, break, continue, and goto. Control flow is straightforward but fundamental.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Declare, define, and call functions. Understand prototypes and header files. Functions are C's primary abstraction.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: Fixed-size contiguous memory blocks. Arrays are the foundation of most data structures in C.

topics.label: Strings
topics.description: Null-terminated char arrays and string.h functions. String handling in C requires care to avoid buffer overflows.

topics.label: Preprocessor Directives
topics.description: #define, #include, #ifdef, #ifndef, and macros. The preprocessor runs before compilation and controls conditional compilation.

topics.label: Header Files & Linking
topics.description: Separate declarations (.h) from implementations (.c). Proper header organization is key to scalable C projects.

core.label: Pointers & Memory
core.description: Pointer arithmetic, dynamic allocation, and memory management.

topics.label: Pointer Basics
topics.description: Understand addresses, dereferencing, and pointer declaration. Pointers are what make C powerful and dangerous.

topics.label: Pointer Arithmetic
topics.description: Increment, decrement, and offset pointers to traverse memory. Essential for array manipulation and buffer processing.

topics.label: Pointers & Arrays
topics.description: Arrays decay to pointers and can be indexed via pointer arithmetic. This duality is central to C idioms.

topics.label: Pointers to Pointers
topics.description: Double pointers for dynamic 2D arrays and modifying pointer arguments. Common in system programming APIs.

topics.label: Dynamic Memory (malloc/free)
topics.description: Allocate heap memory with malloc, calloc, realloc, and release with free. Manual memory management is C's core skill.

topics.label: Memory Leaks & Dangling Pointers
topics.description: Detect and avoid common memory bugs. Leaks and dangling pointers cause crashes and security vulnerabilities.

topics.label: Function Pointers
topics.description: Store and call functions via pointers. Function pointers enable callbacks, plugins, and polymorphism in C.

topics.label: void Pointers
topics.description: Generic pointers that can hold any address. Used in generic data structures and library APIs like qsort.

topics.label: const Correctness
topics.description: Use const with pointers and function parameters to prevent unintended modifications. Improves safety and API clarity.

core.label: Structs, Unions & Enums
core.description: Composite data types and user-defined types.

topics.label: Structs
topics.description: Group related variables into a single type. Structs are C's primary mechanism for creating complex data types.

topics.label: Nested Structs & Arrays of Structs
topics.description: Build hierarchical data with structs containing structs and struct arrays. Models real-world data relationships.

topics.label: Pointers to Structs
topics.description: Access struct members via pointers using the -> operator. Essential for linked data structures and APIs.

topics.label: Typedef
topics.description: Create type aliases for readability. typedef is commonly used with structs and function pointers.

topics.label: Unions
topics.description: Overlay multiple types in the same memory. Unions save memory when only one member is active at a time.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Named integer constants for readability. Enums replace magic numbers and improve code documentation.

topics.label: Bit Fields
topics.description: Pack data into specific bit widths within structs. Used in hardware registers and network protocols.

topics.label: Struct Padding & Alignment
topics.description: Understand how compilers add padding for alignment. Affects struct size and is critical for binary protocols.

core.label: File I/O & Standard Library
core.description: File operations, standard library functions, and error handling.

topics.label: File I/O with stdio
topics.description: fopen, fclose, fread, fwrite, fprintf, fscanf. File I/O is essential for data processing and persistence.

topics.label: Binary File I/O
topics.description: Read and write raw bytes with fread/fwrite. Binary I/O is used for images, databases, and serialization.

topics.label: Error Handling with errno
topics.description: Check errno and use perror/strerror for system call errors. C has no exceptions — errno is the standard error mechanism.

topics.label: String Library (string.h)
topics.description: strcpy, strncpy, strcat, strcmp, strlen, memcpy, memset. Know these functions and their safe alternatives.

topics.label: Math Library
topics.description: sqrt, pow, abs, floor, ceil, and trigonometric functions from math.h. Link with -lm.

topics.label: stdlib Utilities
topics.description: atoi, strtol, rand, srand, qsort, bsearch, exit. General-purpose utilities you'll use constantly.

topics.label: Command-Line Arguments
topics.description: Parse argc and argv to accept program arguments. CLI argument handling is standard for C tools.

topics.label: Assertions
topics.description: Use assert() for debugging invariants. Assertions catch programmer errors during development.

core.label: Data Structures in C
core.description: Implement fundamental data structures using pointers and dynamic memory.

topics.label: Linked Lists
topics.description: Singly and doubly linked lists with dynamic allocation. The classic C data structure exercise.

topics.label: Stacks & Queues
topics.description: Implement stacks (LIFO) and queues (FIFO) with arrays or linked lists. Fundamental for parsing and scheduling.

topics.label: Hash Tables
topics.description: Build a hash map with chaining or open addressing. Hash tables provide O(1) average-case lookups.

topics.label: Binary Trees
topics.description: Implement binary search trees with insert, search, and delete. Trees are used in databases, compilers, and OS kernels.

topics.label: Dynamic Arrays
topics.description: Build a growable array (vector) with realloc. Understanding dynamic arrays helps you appreciate higher-level languages.

topics.label: Graphs
topics.description: Represent graphs with adjacency lists or matrices. Graph algorithms power networking, routing, and social networks.

topics.label: Sorting Algorithms
topics.description: Implement quicksort, mergesort, and understand qsort. Sorting is the most studied family of algorithms.

topics.label: Generic Data Structures with void*
topics.description: Use void pointers and function pointers for type-generic containers. This is how C achieves polymorphism.

core.label: Build Systems & Debugging
core.description: Makefiles, build tools, debugging, and profiling.

topics.label: Makefiles
topics.description: Write Makefiles with targets, dependencies, and variables. Make is the traditional C build tool.

topics.label: CMake
topics.description: Cross-platform build system generation. CMake is the modern standard for C/C++ projects.

topics.label: GDB Debugging
topics.description: Step through code, set breakpoints, inspect variables with GDB. The essential C debugger.

topics.label: Valgrind
topics.description: Detect memory leaks, invalid reads/writes, and use-after-free. Valgrind is indispensable for C development.

topics.label: AddressSanitizer
topics.description: Compile with -fsanitize=address for runtime memory error detection. Faster than Valgrind for many checks.

topics.label: Compiler Warnings
topics.description: Use -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic to catch bugs at compile time. Warnings are your first line of defense.

topics.label: Static Analysis
topics.description: Use tools like cppcheck or clang-tidy to find bugs without running the code. Catches issues compilers miss.

topics.label: Profiling with gprof/perf
topics.description: Find performance bottlenecks with profiling tools. Optimize based on data, not guesses.

core.label: Advanced C & System Programming
core.description: POSIX APIs, concurrency, and advanced language features.

topics.label: POSIX File Descriptors
topics.description: Low-level I/O with open, read, write, close. Understanding file descriptors is key to Unix system programming.

topics.label: Processes (fork/exec)
topics.description: Create child processes with fork and replace them with exec. Foundation of Unix process management.

topics.label: Signals
topics.description: Handle asynchronous events with signal handlers. Signals are used for graceful shutdown and inter-process communication.

topics.label: Pipes & IPC
topics.description: Inter-process communication with pipes, named pipes, and shared memory. Essential for multi-process architectures.

topics.label: Sockets
topics.description: Network programming with TCP/UDP sockets. Sockets are the foundation of all network communication.

topics.label: pthreads
topics.description: POSIX threads for concurrent programming — create, join, mutex, and condition variables.

topics.label: Bitwise Operations
topics.description: AND, OR, XOR, shifts, and bit masks. Used in embedded systems, protocols, and performance-critical code.

topics.label: Volatile & Restrict
topics.description: Understand volatile for hardware registers and restrict for optimization hints. Important for embedded and performance.

topics.label: Undefined Behavior
topics.description: Recognize and avoid undefined behavior — signed overflow, null deref, strict aliasing. UB is C's biggest pitfall.
